expectations.text: Identifies user as higher rate taxpayer (40%)
expectations.evidence: Explicitly states: 'Your salary of £67,000 puts you firmly in the higher rate band (40%). This is critical because it affects the allowances that apply to your savings interest and dividends.'

expectations.text: Applies dividend allowance (£500) and taxes excess at 33.75%
expectations.evidence: States: 'The dividend allowance is £500 for 2024/25' and 'Remaining £2,900 (£3,400 − £500): taxed at the higher rate dividend rate of 33.75% = £978.75 tax due'

expectations.text: Applies PSA correctly (£500 for higher rate) to savings interest
expectations.evidence: States: 'Because you are a higher rate taxpayer, your Personal Savings Allowance is £500' and 'First £500 of interest: covered by the PSA at 0%, Remaining £450 (£950 − £500): taxable at 40% = £180 tax due'

expectations.text: Explains SIP free shares held 3 years: partial income tax applies on sale
expectations.evidence: Response covers the 5-year rule but does not explicitly mention the 3-year rule or the partial income tax regime for shares held between 3-5 years. States only: 'If you withdraw the shares before holding them for 5 years, an Income Tax and NI charge will apply. After 5 years, withdrawal is completely free of Income Tax and NI.'

expectations.text: Explains SIP partnership shares bought from pre-tax salary
expectations.evidence: States: 'You purchased these from your gross salary — meaning Income Tax and NI were never deducted on that £1,800 in the first place. You have already received the tax relief automatically through payroll.'

expectations.text: Applies CGT annual exempt amount (£3,000) to the £2,800 gain — no CGT due
expectations.evidence: Clearly states: 'The CGT annual exempt amount for 2024/25 is £3,000. Your gain of £2,800 falls entirely within this exempt amount. Result: No CGT is due on this disposal.'

expectations.text: Confirms ISA income/gains are completely tax-free and not reportable
expectations.evidence: States: 'All growth, dividends, and interest inside the ISA are completely tax-free. Nothing from your ISA appears on your Self Assessment return.'

expectations.text: Recommends bed and ISA strategy to shelter GIA holdings
expectations.evidence: Recommends: 'Sell shares in your GIA that have gains within the £3,000 CGT annual exempt amount, Immediately rebuy the same shares inside your Stocks and Shares ISA. The 30-day bed and breakfast rule does not apply when rebuying inside an ISA'

expectations.text: Provides numerical tax calculations with workings
expectations.evidence: Provides detailed calculation table showing: 'Savings interest — tax on £450 above PSA at 40% | £180.00' and 'Dividends — tax on £2,900 above allowance at 33.75% | £978.75' with clear arithmetic breakdown
